entities.name: HYPER-LOCALIZED FERTILIZER ADVISORY SYSTEM
entities.description: A system providing field-level, data-driven guidance for nutrient application to optimize fertilizer use.

entities.name: PRECISION NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: An agricultural management strategy that applies nutrients at the right time, place, and rate.

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the agricultural practices and smallholder farmers are located.

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers in Ethiopia who manage small-scale agricultural plots.

entities.name: UNIFORM FERTILIZER RECOMMENDATIONS
entities.description: The practice of applying the same fertilizer guidelines across diverse agricultural areas.

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The earth's surface layer which exhibits strong variation in nutrient levels.

entities.name: CLIMATE
entities.description: Atmospheric conditions that are variable and influence crop growth and fertilizer needs.

entities.name: TOPOGRAPHY
entities.description: The physical features of the land that vary across agricultural areas.

entities.name: FERTILIZER
entities.description: Substances added to soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth.

entities.name: NUTRIENT RUNOFF
entities.description: The movement of excess fertilizer from the soil into water bodies.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in environmental quality resulting from nutrient runoff.

entities.name: ECONOMIC LOSSES
entities.description: Financial deficits incurred by farmers due to inefficient fertilizer application.

entities.name: YIELDS
entities.description: The measure of agricultural productivity per unit of land.
